name: Yew
description: A Rust/Wasm framework for creating reliable and efficient web applications with a React-inspired component architecture

description.value: A Rust/Wasm framework for creating reliable and efficient web applications with a React-inspired component architecture

initial-release.comment: First public release in 2017; one of the earliest Rust web frameworks

github-stars.comment: ~32.2k stars as of December 2025; one of the most starred Rust web frameworks

active-maintenance.comment: Active development with 2,734 commits, 437 contributors; regular releases

reactivity-model.comment: Uses Virtual DOM diffing; component re-renders when state changes and diffs against previous output

component-model.comment: Function components with hooks (use_state, use_effect, etc.); also supports struct components with Component trait

rendering-paradigm.comment: Declarative html! macro with JSX-like syntax using Rust expressions

state-management-approach.comment: Built-in hooks (use_state, use_reducer, use_context); Yewdux recommended for complex global state

opinionatedness.comment: Minimal opinions; provides core component model, leaves routing/state/styling to user choice

backward-compatibility.comment: Not 1.0 yet; documentation notes 'Be prepared to do major refactoring due to breaking API changes'

primary-language.comment: Written in Rust (96.9%); JavaScript used only for interop

compiles-to.comment: Compiles Rust to WebAssembly via wasm32-unknown-unknown target

typescript-support.comment: Rust framework; uses Rust's type system instead of TypeScript

template-syntax.comment: html! procedural macro with JSX-like syntax; developers familiar with React/JSX will feel at home

styling-approach.comment: No built-in styling; works with external CSS, Tailwind, or community crates like stylist

build-required.comment: Requires Rust toolchain with wasm32-unknown-unknown target; Trunk is the recommended build tool

wasm-interop.comment: Uses wasm-bindgen for JavaScript interop; supports NPM packages and existing JavaScript integration

reactive-primitives.comment: use_state hook creates reactive state; use_reducer for complex state logic

computed-values.comment: use_memo hook for memoized computed values

two-way-binding.comment: No built-in two-way binding; requires explicit event handling and state updates like React

event-handling.comment: Declarative event binding in html! macro (onclick, oninput, etc.)

conditional-rendering.comment: Conditional rendering via if/else and match expressions in html! macro

list-rendering.comment: Iterator-based list rendering in html! macro; similar to React's map pattern

component-slots.comment: children prop for content projection via Html type or ChildrenRenderer

teleport-portal.comment: Portal component available via yew-portal crate for rendering outside component tree

suspense.comment: <Suspense> component for async loading boundaries with fallback UI

error-boundaries.comment: No built-in error boundary component; errors typically handled via Result types

context-provide-inject.comment: use_context hook for accessing provided context; ContextProvider component for injection

refs-direct-dom-access.comment: use_node_ref hook for direct DOM element access via NodeRef

official-router.comment: yew-router is the official first-party routing solution

nested-routes.comment: Support for nested routes via Switch component and nested route enums

dynamic-routes.comment: Parameterized routes with path parameters defined in Route enum

route-guards.comment: Navigation guards via listener hooks on BrowserRouter

lazy-loading-routes.comment: WASM binary splitting for routes not built-in; requires manual implementation

scroll-restoration.comment: Scroll restoration available via location state and browser history API

built-in-state.comment: use_state and use_reducer hooks for local component state

built-in-global-state.comment: Context API (ContextProvider + use_context) for app-wide state

global-state-library.comment: Yewdux is the recommended external state management library for complex scenarios

devtools-state-inspection.comment: yew-debugger project exists but not official; no mature browser devtools extension

state-persistence.comment: No built-in persistence; available via community crates or browser storage APIs

time-travel-debugging.comment: Not supported; no official devtools with time-travel capabilities

official-cli.comment: No official CLI; Trunk is the recommended third-party build tool for Yew projects

hot-module-replacement.comment: Trunk provides live reload and rebuilds on file changes via trunk serve

browser-devtools.comment: No official browser devtools extension; yew-debugger is community-developed

vs-code-extension.comment: No official VS Code extension; rust-analyzer provides general Rust support but no html! macro autocomplete

error-messages.comment: Rust compiler errors are helpful; html! macro errors can be cryptic but improving

documentation-quality.comment: Good documentation with tutorials, API reference on docs.rs, multilingual support (EN, JP, ZH)

playground.comment: No official online playground due to Rust/WASM compilation requirements

bundle-size-core.comment: ~200 kB WASM gzipped for minimal app; WASM has excellent memory footprint but larger initial load than JS

bundle-size-full.comment: ~350 kB WASM gzipped with router; binary size reduction is a roadmap priority

virtual-dom.comment: Uses Virtual DOM diffing; minimizes DOM API calls for each render

compile-time-optimization.comment: Rust compiler performs extensive optimizations; wasm-opt further optimizes WASM binaries

tree-shaking.comment: Rust's dead code elimination; LTO (Link Time Optimization) recommended for production

code-splitting.comment: No built-in WASM binary splitting; entire app compiles to single WASM file

lazy-components.comment: No built-in lazy loading; components are part of single WASM binary

ssr-capable.comment: SSR support via ssr feature flag with ServerRenderer; works with hydration

streaming-ssr.comment: Renders to string; no streaming SSR support

partial-hydration.comment: Full hydration only; no islands architecture or partial hydration support

js-framework-benchmark.comment: Estimated ~1.25 geometric mean; good performance but behind Leptos/Dioxus due to Virtual DOM overhead

npm-weekly-downloads.comment: Rust crate, not npm package; 3.4M total downloads and 1.2M recent downloads on crates.io

meta-framework.comment: No meta-framework; typically used with Axum/Actix for server-side or standalone for SPA

major-companies-using.comment: Used by Rust enthusiasts; Theirstack tracks 4 companies using Yew but names not public

official-sponsor-backer.comment: Community-driven project maintained by yewstack organization; no major corporate backing

stack-overflow-questions.comment: Estimated ~200 questions tagged with 'yew'; community primarily uses Discord and GitHub

job-market-demand.comment: Niche; primarily used by Rust developers and teams; limited job postings

official-testing-library.comment: No official testing library; testing support is work in progress

testing-library-name.comment: Recommended to use wasm_bindgen_test crate for unit tests; fantoccini for E2E

component-testing.comment: Component testing via wasm-pack test; limited by inability to create ComponentLinks externally

e2e-testing-integration.comment: E2E testing possible via fantoccini (Selenium for Rust) or other WebDriver tools

mobile-framework.comment: Works with Tauri 2.0 for mobile (iOS/Android); rust-yew-axum-tauri-desktop template available

pwa-support.comment: yew-pwa-minimal template available; can be configured as PWA

desktop-framework.comment: Excellent Tauri integration; multiple templates and examples available

learning-curve.comment: Steep learning curve; requires Rust knowledge plus React-like concepts adapted to Rust's ownership model

concepts-to-learn.comment: Rust ownership, html! macro, components, hooks, context, routing, agents, WASM concepts, build tooling (Trunk)

time-to-hello-world.comment: ~10 minutes with Rust toolchain installed; includes Trunk install and basic app setup

migration-path.comment: No migration guides from JS frameworks; requires rewrite in Rust

incremental-adoption.comment: Can be used alongside JavaScript via wasm-pack; WASM output can integrate with existing JS apps
